examples.title: Air Quality Index Calculation
examples.description: Algorithm to calculate AQI based on pollutant concentrations
examples.code:
ALGORITHM Calculate_AQI
INPUT: PM25_conc, PM10_conc, SO2_conc, NO2_conc, O3_conc
OUTPUT: AQI_value, Category

BEGIN
 // Calculate individual AQI for each pollutant
 PM25_AQI = (PM25_conc / 35.4) * 100
 PM10_AQI = (PM10_conc / 154) * 100
 SO2_AQI = (SO2_conc / 75) * 100
 NO2_AQI = (NO2_conc / 100) * 100
 O3_AQI = (O3_conc / 54) * 100
 
 // AQI is the maximum among all pollutants
 AQI_value = MAX(PM25_AQI, PM10_AQI, SO2_AQI, NO2_AQI, O3_AQI)
 
 // Determine category
 IF AQI_value <= 50 THEN
 Category = "Good"
 ELSE IF AQI_value <= 100 THEN
 Category = "Moderate"
 ELSE IF AQI_value <= 150 THEN
 Category = "Unhealthy for Sensitive Groups"
 ELSE IF AQI_value <= 200 THEN
 Category = "Unhealthy"
 ELSE IF AQI_value <= 300 THEN
 Category = "Very Unhealthy"
 ELSE
 Category = "Hazardous"
 END IF
 
 RETURN AQI_value, Category
END
examples.explanation: This algorithm calculates AQI by measuring individual pollutant concentrations and taking the maximum value. It then categorizes the air quality from Good to Hazardous based on standard EPA breakpoints.

examples.title: BOD Calculation Algorithm
examples.description: Algorithm to calculate Biochemical Oxygen Demand for water sample
examples.code:
ALGORITHM Calculate_BOD
INPUT: initial_DO, final_DO, dilution_factor, volume_sample
OUTPUT: BOD_value

BEGIN
 // DO = Dissolved Oxygen in mg/L
 // Initial DO measured immediately after collection
 // Final DO measured after 5 days incubation at 20°C
 
 DO_initial = initial_DO // mg/L
 DO_final = final_DO // mg/L
 
 // Calculate BOD using formula
 BOD_raw = (DO_initial - DO_final) * dilution_factor
 
 // Apply temperature correction for non-standard conditions
 temperature = 20 // assumed incubation temperature in °C
 correction_factor = 1.0 + 0.02 * (temperature - 20)
 
 BOD_value = BOD_raw * correction_factor
 
 // Classify water quality
 IF BOD_value < 1 THEN
 Quality = "Clean"
 ELSE IF BOD_value < 3 THEN
 Quality = "Moderately Polluted"
 ELSE IF BOD_value < 8 THEN
 Quality = "Polluted"
 ELSE
 Quality = "Heavily Polluted"
 END IF
 
 RETURN BOD_value, Quality
END
examples.explanation: BOD measures oxygen required by microorganisms to decompose organic matter. Higher BOD indicates more pollution. The algorithm accounts for dilution factor and temperature correction.

examples.title: Pollution Monitoring System
examples.description: Simple monitoring system to track pollution levels and alert
examples.code:
CLASS PollutionMonitor
ATTRIBUTES:
 location_name
 sensor_data[] // array of pollutant readings
 threshold_values[] // maximum allowable limits
 alert_flag

METHODS:
 PROCEDURE ReadSensors()
 BEGIN
 sensor_data[0] = Read_PM25()
 sensor_data[1] = Read_PM10()
 sensor_data[2] = Read_SO2()
 sensor_data[3] = Read_NO2()
 END PROCEDURE

 PROCEDURE CheckThresholds()
 BEGIN
 alert_flag = FALSE
 FOR i = 0 TO 3 DO
 IF sensor_data[i] > threshold_values[i] THEN
 alert_flag = TRUE
 DISPLAY "Alert: " + pollutant_names[i] + " exceeds limit!"
 END IF
 END FOR
 END PROCEDURE

 PROCEDURE GenerateReport()
 BEGIN
 DISPLAY "Location: " + location_name
 DISPLAY "Pollution Levels:"
 FOR i = 0 TO 3 DO
 DISPLAY pollutant_names[i] + ": " + sensor_data[i]
 END FOR
 IF alert_flag THEN
 DISPLAY "STATUS: ALERT - Action Required"
 ELSE
 DISPLAY "STATUS: Normal - Within Limits"
 END IF
 END PROCEDURE

// Main Program
BEGIN
 monitor = NEW PollutionMonitor("City Center")
 monitor.ReadSensors()
 monitor.CheckThresholds()
 monitor.GenerateReport()
END
examples.explanation: This pseudocode demonstrates a basic pollution monitoring system that reads pollutant sensors, compares them with threshold values, and generates alerts when pollution exceeds safe limits.
